templates.title: Unpaid wages preparation checklist
templates.guidance_label: General preparation guidance; applicable law and deadlines must be retrieved separately.

items.label: Collect appointment letters, contracts, identity cards, attendance records, or other proof of work.
Keep payslips, wage statements, bank entries, and records of partial payments.
Write a dated chronology of work performed, pay periods, amounts expected, and amounts received.
Preserve messages or emails about wages, payment promises, deductions, or termination.
Record the employer's legal name, workplace address, supervisor, and available contact details.
Keep original records unchanged and work from copies when sharing documents.

templates.title: FIR or legal notice preparation checklist
templates.guidance_label: General document-preparation guidance; urgency, applicable law, and response dates require verified review.

items.label: Capture every page, including stamps, signatures, annexures, and the reverse side where used.
Record the FIR, diary, case, notice, or reference number exactly as printed.
Record the issuing police station, court, department, lawyer, or other named authority.
Preserve the envelope or delivery record and note when and how the document was received.
Prepare a factual chronology and separate what you directly know from what others allege.
Seek prompt qualified human help when the document mentions arrest, detention, appearance, a hearing, or an expiring response period.

templates.title: Rental security-deposit preparation checklist
templates.guidance_label: General preparation guidance; tenancy-law applicability depends on jurisdiction and facts.

items.label: Keep the complete rent, lease, licence, or occupancy agreement and any renewal documents.
Collect receipts, bank entries, or written acknowledgements showing the deposit amount and date.
Record the property address, tenancy dates, monthly payment, and the names used in the agreement.
Preserve dated move-in and move-out photographs, videos, inventories, and inspection records.
Keep proof of key handover, possession return, meter readings, and final payment reconciliation.
Preserve refund requests, replies, claimed deductions, estimates, invoices, and settlement proposals.
